title: Time zone Override
excerpt: In the profile settings it’s possible to set your time zone manual. However the computer time zone will override this time zone, making this setting quit wasted.  To get around this, the only way to choose another time z…

posts.author: arild (Arild Bekkelund)
posts.cookedHtml:
In the profile settings it’s possible to set your time zone manual. However the computer time zone will override this time zone, making this setting quit wasted. To get around this, the only way to choose another time zone than your local time zone, is to change your computer time zone. This is highly impractical for all of us that live in another time zone than our working time zone.
My suggestion is to change this setting to "automatic time zone (computer time zone) or manual time zone, that will override the computer time zone. The same time zone settings should also apply on column level.

Hope this make sense.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @arild

Until recently the only TZ that was used by monday was the TX setting in the user profile. A few month ago this is changed to use the device TZ. In my opinion this is a bad decision. You probably need to issue a feature request to get back to the previous situation

posts.author: inspiredearth (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
From my perspective, this is totally nuts. A potential nightmare for people who travel internationally whilst working.

I’ve just wasted a couple of hours trying to figure out why I am getting totally unexpected results from date column API requests to Monday.

I assumed the date/time I set in the Monday board was the time zone of the business (which is the timezone on my profile). Yet I was getting something totally different. Reading this Feature Request is the first thing I’ve found explaining what is likely going on.

posts.author: smithjohn (smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi I can relate to the time zone override issue sometimes it gets tricky coordinating tasks across different regions especially when deadlines are time sensitive. One thing thats helped me is keeping a consistent reference schedule for the team and syncing it with calendar tools to reduce confusion. I also noticed that resources like transport services can be surprisingly helpful for visualizing schedules and planning timelines across zones Have you tried using any automation or notification rules in Monday to adjust tasks based on each users time zone? It might make things a bit smoother.
